title: Implement trigger-based agency controller
summary: Replace the current agency planning stub with a bounded trigger-based controller whose names match user-facing moments such as task end, sprint end, roadmap end, and budget end.

goal.outcome: Users can choose understandable agency triggers and safe autonomy budgets instead of opaque autonomy levels or OpenClaw-derived agency-loop terminology.
acceptance: Agency trigger names align with user-facing events: task-end, sprint-end, roadmap-end, and budget-end.
Each trigger maps to bounded observe/maintain/work behavior with explicit budgets and stop conditions.
Cron-like operation is limited to read-only or low-risk drift/audit maintenance unless explicit policy permits more.
Agency can route to feedback/documentation/implementation/validation only when trigger, budget, and risk policy allow it.
Old agency-loop terminology is removed from live KB, source, skills, README, and smoke tests.
non_goals: Do not implement unbounded autonomous coding.
Do not push, release, archive, or mutate high-risk state without explicit approval and green checks.
Do not keep queue.json as a generated queue lens; graph owns derived queue order.
verification: Add or update smoke coverage for agency tool registration and trigger-to-action planning.
Verify live KB/source/docs contain no old agency-loop terminology and queue.json is not regenerated.

delta.desired: Agency planning is trigger-aware, graph-driven, and free of OpenClaw-derived agency-loop terminology. Graph owns derived queue order; roadmap owns work truth.
delta.current: planAgency accepts trigger parameter; codewiki_agency is the Pi-facing tool; state builders expose agency lanes; queue.json generation removed; KB clarifies agency and graph/roadmap boundaries.
delta.closure: Agency terminology and trigger-based planning implemented; queue.json removed; implementation build 2026-05-10-task-038-agency-controller.json validates closure.
